name: Charge port door position
comment: Filtered door position sensor counts, as a percentage — more granular than the open/closed state machine
accuracyComment: From leaked Tesla internal signal DB, unverified. This message is actually multiplexed (a CP_sensorDataSelect byte picks between door/cover/presence readings) — this entry assumes it's always reporting door position, which is only correct when that selector happens to be in the DOOR state

decode.note: Multiplexed message, only correctly decoded when CP_sensorDataSelect==DOOR — treat as unreliable until the multiplexer is handled properly

comment: Only meaningful while a charge cable's pilot signal is being sensed

name: Cell voltage block selector (0x754)
comment: SMT uses 0x754 as the cell-voltage block staging message (works WITH 0x51E). This is likely the real per-cell voltage path on your car — different from the 0x6F2 path other Model S years use. Needs the paired 0x51E frames and multiplexed decode to fully resolve.
accuracyComment: Translated directly from Scan My Tesla's open-source ModelSPackets.cs — the actual reference app's own decode logic

decode.note: Block selector byte. Full per-cell decode requires pairing with 0x51E frames (multiplexed) — see Scan My Tesla source. This is a lead toward real cell data on YOUR car, not a finished decode

name: 0x138 (observed, unmapped)
comment: OBSERVED but NOT decoded. Length 5. Bytes 1-2 vary across captures (11/1F, 47/55). Present both parked and moving. Slowly-changing candidate value in bytes 1-2.
accuracyComment: Recorded from real on-car captures — byte behavior noted, but no signal identity/scale/unit is known. Not in any community source (SMT/BogGyver/talas9). Use Map Unknown Signal on-car to identify.
source: Observed in user's own CAN captures (unidentified)

name: 0x148 (observed, unmapped)
comment: OBSERVED but NOT decoded. Length 8. Bytes 0-5 stable at rest; BYTE 4 changes with motion (A9 parked -> B3 at 0.5mph) = strong candidate for a speed/RPM/torque value. Bytes 6-7 = rolling counter+checksum (don't decode). Byte 2 wobbles slowly (temp/voltage?).
accuracyComment: Recorded from real on-car captures — byte behavior noted, but no signal identity/scale/unit is known. Not in any community source (SMT/BogGyver/talas9). Use Map Unknown Signal on-car to identify.
source: Observed in user's own CAN captures (unidentified)

name: 0x158 (observed, unmapped)
comment: OBSERVED but NOT decoded. Length 8 when moving (truncates at rest). Many bytes active during motion = motion-related. Needs steady-speed capture to isolate which byte tracks speed vs RPM.
accuracyComment: Recorded from real on-car captures — byte behavior noted, but no signal identity/scale/unit is known. Not in any community source (SMT/BogGyver/talas9). Use Map Unknown Signal on-car to identify.
source: Observed in user's own CAN captures (unidentified)

name: 0x178 (observed, unmapped)
comment: OBSERVED but NOT decoded. Length 8. CORRECTION from earlier note: NOT just a counter. Reads all-zeros when parked, but bytes 0,2,3,5,6 come ALIVE when moving = genuine drive/motion signal. Map on-car while varying speed.
accuracyComment: Recorded from real on-car captures — byte behavior noted, but no signal identity/scale/unit is known. Not in any community source (SMT/BogGyver/talas9). Use Map Unknown Signal on-car to identify.
source: Observed in user's own CAN captures (unidentified)

name: 0x185 (observed, unmapped)
comment: OBSERVED but NOT decoded. Length 8. CORRECTION: like 0x178, dead at rest but active when moving (bytes 0-5 populate) = real drive-related signal, not a counter. Map on-car under motion.
accuracyComment: Recorded from real on-car captures — byte behavior noted, but no signal identity/scale/unit is known. Not in any community source (SMT/BogGyver/talas9). Use Map Unknown Signal on-car to identify.
source: Observed in user's own CAN captures (unidentified)

name: Speed (0x256 IC)
comment: wk057: 12-bit, 0.1 MPH, always sent as MPH. bytes 2 + low nibble of byte 3

decode.note: wk057 primary source. This is the IC analog-bar speed. Cross-check against 0x116 vehicleSpeed

accuracyComment: From wk057 (Jason Hughes) primary Model S CAN document — the authoritative original source, same author underlying Scan My Tesla
source: wk057/skie.net Tesla Model S CAN Deciphering v0.1

name: Numeric display speed
comment: wk057 byte6: the numeric speed shown on IC (MPH or KM/H per byte3 bit7)

decode.note: wk057 primary source. Unit follows the car's display setting
